Class BinOpAgent

java.lang.Object
configs.BinOpAgent
All Implemented Interfaces:
Agent
public class BinOpAgent extends Object implements Agent
Generic two-input binary-operation agent.

Subscribes to two input topics and applies a BinaryOperator when both inputs have valid numeric values, publishing the result to an output topic. The agent name is "A" + baseName (e.g. "Aplus").

Unlike PlusAgent, this agent auto-subscribes and registers as a publisher in its constructor via TopicManagerSingleton.

Thread safety: callback(java.lang.String, graph.Message) is synchronized.

See Also:
  • Agent
  • PlusAgent
  • MathExampleConfig

  • Constructor Details

    • BinOpAgent

      public BinOpAgent(String baseName, String in1, String in2, String out, BinaryOperator<Double> op)
      Creates a binary-operation agent and wires it to the topic manager.

      Subscribes to in1 and in2, registers as publisher on out.

      Parameters:
      baseName - the base name used to form the agent name ("A" + baseName)
      in1 - the first input topic name
      in2 - the second input topic name
      out - the output topic name
      op - the binary operation to apply when both inputs are available

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the agent name.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface Agent
      Returns:
      "A" concatenated with the base name provided at construction

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Clears cached input values to Double.NaN.
      Specified by:
      reset in interface Agent

    • close

      public void close()
      Unsubscribes from input topics and removes publisher registration.
      Specified by:
      close in interface Agent

    • callback

      public void callback(String topic, Message msg)
      Accumulates input values and publishes the result of the binary operation.

      Publishes only when both val1 and val2 are valid numbers.

      Specified by:
      callback in interface Agent
      Parameters:
      topic - the topic that published the message
      msg - the incoming message
